nodes.name: _frequency_rows

nodes.name: _co_occurrence_pairs
nodes.summary: Mines co-occurring event pairs within sessions and returns ranked pair counts with support/confidence-shaped metrics.

nodes.name: open_events_db

nodes.name: _validate_schema_version
nodes.summary: Reads the schema_version row from the meta table and raises if it does not match the constant expected by this code path.

nodes.name: query_q1_longest_by_skill
nodes.summary: Q1: per-skill longest invocations — surfaces skills whose calls run unusually long compared to their typical duration.

nodes.name: query_q2_model_overkill
nodes.summary: Q2: model-overkill — flags calls that used a heavyweight model on a task whose downstream verdict says a lighter model would have sufficed.

nodes.name: query_q3_dag_subagent_cost
nodes.summary: Q3: DAG sub-agent cost rollup — sums duration/cost across a session's parent/child agent tree.

nodes.name: query_q4_background_failure
nodes.summary: Q4: background-task failure rate — flags tasks that ran in background mode and finished with non-success verdicts.

nodes.name: query_q5_gate_effectiveness
nodes.summary: Q5: per-gate effectiveness — joins gate evaluations with subsequent outcomes to produce correlation-only signals per stable 12-char gate_id.

nodes.name: query_q6_time_of_day_sessions
nodes.summary: Q6: time-of-day session profile — buckets sessions by hour-of-day to surface working-rhythm patterns.

nodes.name: query_q7_tool_server_bottlenecks
nodes.summary: Q7: tool/server bottlenecks — ranks MCP servers by total invocation duration to surface latency hot-spots.

nodes.name: query_q8_frustration_pairs
nodes.summary: Q8: frustration pairs — mines event pairs immediately preceding negative-verdict outcomes as proxy frustration signals.

nodes.name: query_q9_drift
nodes.summary: Q9: drift — compares recent skill performance against a baseline window to detect regression or improvement.

nodes.name: query_q10_eval_verdict_roi
nodes.summary: Q10: eval-verdict ROI — aggregates judge verdicts to estimate the return-on-investment of running the eval pipeline.

nodes.name: query_gate_with_evidence
nodes.summary: Returns the evidence rows associated with a single gate so the dashboard can show a per-gate evidence drill-down.

nodes.name: query_dag_parent_child_cost
nodes.summary: Builds a parent/child cost breakdown across a session's DAG for the dashboard's nested-cost view.

nodes.name: query_cache_hit_ratio
nodes.summary: Computes prompt-cache hit ratios by skill/session to surface cache effectiveness for cost optimisation.

nodes.name: load_samples_rows
nodes.summary: Loads sample event rows used by analyst smoke tests and the dashboard recent-events panel.

nodes.name: analyst_score.py
nodes.summary: Analyst subcommand that scores skill performance from events (with a no-events fallback path), pulling weights from a YAML profile to drive the analyst scoring artifact.

nodes.name: artifact_writer.py
nodes.summary: Contract-driven artifact persistence layer: loads contracts/*.yaml, resolves output templates per (state_root, repo_id), serialises payloads as JSON/JSONL/Markdown/text, and writes atomically via state_paths.
nodes.languageNotes: Refuses to write through symlinks; serialisation dispatch is keyed by contract format and never falls back silently.

nodes.name: ce_playbook.py
nodes.summary: Renders a Claude-Engine playbook section for session-context.md — picks a persona, annotates skill usage, and decorates with extra CE skills if CE is installed locally.

nodes.name: render_state_surface.py
nodes.summary: Top-level renderer that materialises the durable agent-facing surfaces (Markdown, HTML, JSON, per-session reports) from the read-only state via alc_query; also handles session-report rotation.

nodes.name: _load_outcome_weights
nodes.summary: Loads outcome-weight YAML configuration (kind to weight) used to score skill performance, with sensible defaults when missing.

nodes.name: _score_rows
nodes.summary: Applies outcome weights to raw event rows to produce per-skill scored rows.

nodes.name: _run_from_events
nodes.summary: Primary scoring path that runs when the v4 events DB is present and produces the analyst scoring artifact directly from events.

nodes.name: _run_fallback
nodes.summary: Fallback scoring path used when no events DB exists yet — emits a minimal contract-compliant artifact based on recommendations only.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Dispatcher that picks _run_from_events or _run_fallback based on whether the events DB exists.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint that parses args, invokes run, and prints the JSON outcome summary.

nodes.name: _validate_entry
nodes.summary: Validates a contract YAML entry's required keys, ID shape, format whitelist, and template placeholder usage; raises on any contract violation.

nodes.name: load_contracts
nodes.summary: Loads every YAML file in the contracts tree and returns the validated entries keyed by contract ID.

nodes.name: merged_registry
nodes.summary: Merges contract entries from multiple sources into a single registry, detecting and rejecting ID collisions.

nodes.name: _state_root
nodes.summary: Resolves the state root for artifact writes, applying the documented precedence and emitting clear errors when the path is unsafe.

nodes.name: _resolve_template
nodes.summary: Substitutes repo_id / state_root placeholders in a contract template into a concrete output path.

nodes.name: _artifact_path
nodes.summary: Computes the final absolute path for an artifact, refusing symlinked or non-regular targets per the trust model.

nodes.name: _serialize_jsonl
nodes.summary: Serialises a list payload into JSON Lines, applying field ordering and rejecting non-dict rows.

nodes.name: _serialize
nodes.summary: Dispatches a payload to the right serialiser based on the contract format field, raising on unknown formats.

nodes.name: write_artifact
nodes.summary: Public entrypoint: looks up the contract by ID, resolves the path, serialises the payload, and writes atomically via state_paths.atomic_write_text.

nodes.name: transcript_parser.py

nodes.name: _parse_since
nodes.summary: Parses a human-friendly `--since` argument (digits, `7d`, `12h`, `30m`, `90s`) into a `datetime.timedelta`, raising ValueError on malformed input.

nodes.name: _transcript_files
nodes.summary: Recursively enumerates `.jsonl` and `.json` transcript files under a root path, deduplicates by resolved path, and returns them sorted by mtime for deterministic ingestion order.

nodes.name: backfill
nodes.summary: Walks Claude and Codex transcript trees, filters to files newer than the cutoff, parses each into rows, stamps deterministic event IDs, and writes the merged batch through the event writer.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint that parses arguments, resolves the state root, invokes `backfill`, and prints a short human-readable summary with the count of new rows written.

nodes.name: _telemetry_rollup
nodes.summary: Combines a start and end event's telemetry into a single rolled-up record — computing duration_ms from timestamps and summing optional int/float fields like tokens and cost.

nodes.name: _derive_pair_row
nodes.summary: Produces a single derived event row from a matched start/end pair, deriving its deterministic event ID, actor info, telemetry rollup, and correlation chain.

nodes.name: _pair_events
nodes.summary: Streams rows in order while maintaining per-correlation-key FIFO queues of pending tool starts and subagent dispatches, emitting derived pair rows whenever a matching end arrives.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI driver that loads existing event IDs, replays the event log through the pair-matcher, deduplicates against what is already persisted, writes derived rows, and warns about unmatched starts.

nodes.name: event_emit
nodes.summary: Programmatic entry point that builds a canonical EventV4 row from positional inputs (kind, actor name/kind, optional parent and payload) and delegates the durable write to `event_writer.write_event`.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI wrapper that decodes the JSON payload from argv, calls `event_emit`, and prints the resulting event ID for the caller's logs.

nodes.name: ExecScope
nodes.summary: String-backed Enum naming the three sandbox tiers — read, worktree, eval — that gate which commands and paths are allowed.

nodes.name: ExecResult
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass carrying the outcome of a sandboxed command — exit code, captured stdout/stderr paths, duration, event id, optional worktree dir, and timed_out flag.

nodes.name: _contains_parent_path_traversal
nodes.summary: Scans command tokens for `..` segments and other parent-path-traversal patterns, used to deny sandboxed commands that would escape the allowed root.

nodes.name: _sanitize_env_for_sandbox
nodes.summary: Builds the environment dict passed to subprocess.run, stripping credential-bearing variables and adding sandbox-specific markers.

nodes.name: _spawn_worktree
nodes.summary: Creates an ephemeral git worktree directory used as the cwd for worktree-scope and eval-scope sandbox runs so mutations stay isolated.

nodes.name: _run_in_shell
nodes.summary: Launches the user command as a child process with bounded timeout, captures stdout/stderr to disk, and returns timing + exit-code data for the caller.

nodes.name: _build_event_payload
nodes.summary: Assembles the durable EventV4 payload that describes a sandbox run — scope, exit code, truncated outputs, run id — within the event payload byte cap.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Central sandbox orchestrator that validates the command and scope, sets up the worktree if needed, executes under timeout, captures outputs, emits the durable event, and returns a fully populated ExecResult.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint for `exec_sandbox` — parses args, builds an ExecScope and actor record, invokes `run`, and surfaces the structured result to the caller.

nodes.name: _read_cursor
nodes.summary: Loads the last-seen mtime cursor from disk so the next ingestion pass only processes transcripts modified after the previous run.

nodes.name: _transcript_files
nodes.summary: Enumerates session transcript files under a root path, deduplicating by resolved path and sorting by mtime for stable ingestion order.

nodes.name: ingest
nodes.summary: Reads the mtime cursor, parses just the transcripts newer than that cursor, batches the rows through the event writer, and persists the updated cursor on success.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint that parses Claude/Codex paths and the state dir, then invokes `ingest` and reports how many new rows were written.

nodes.name: iter_transcript_jsonl
nodes.summary: Lazily yields decoded JSON records from a JSONL transcript file, skipping blank and malformed lines so a single corrupt line does not abort ingestion.

nodes.name: _normalize_path

nodes.name: _extract_event_id

nodes.summary: Runs a bounded shell command via the sandbox module in read/worktree/eval scope and returns the stringified stdout/stderr to the MCP client.

nodes.name: _write_stdout_message

nodes.name: alc-reviewer.md

nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with YAML frontmatter declaring agent name, description (with usage examples), model inheritance, and color — the canonical Claude Code subagent persona format.

nodes.name: claude.yaml
nodes.summary: Claude runtime registration manifest for the agent-learning-compounder plugin: declares display name, default prompt, implicit-invocation policy, and registers the alc-reviewer subagent persona.

nodes.name: openai.yaml
nodes.summary: OpenAI/Codex runtime registration manifest for the agent-learning-compounder plugin: mirrors the Claude manifest, declaring display name, default prompt, implicit-invocation policy, and the alc-reviewer subagent persona.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Main dashboard shell that loads HTMX from CDN and defines three sections (gates, queue, probes) that auto-refresh every 30 seconds by fetching partial templates.
nodes.languageNotes: HTMX-driven layout: each <section> declares hx-get to a partial endpoint with hx-trigger='load, every 30s' for progressive enhancement without client-side JS.

nodes.name: _gates.html
nodes.summary: HTMX partial that renders the approved-gates table by parsing the `gates_md` markdown stream line-by-line into domain, category, gate ID, and gate description columns.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Jinja2 namespace() to maintain mutable parser state across the for-loop while transforming flat markdown lines into structured rows.

nodes.name: _queue.html
nodes.summary: HTMX partial that renders the improvement-queue table with ID, kind, text, and timestamp columns, falling back to an 'Empty queue.' row when no items are pending.

nodes.name: _probes.html
nodes.summary: HTMX partial that renders the active causal-probe table mapping gate IDs to their skip-rate percentages, with a fallback row when no probes are active.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entrypoint for the ALC dashboard SPA; mounts the React root and ships an inline `alc-payload` JSON script tag that the server populates with snapshot data at render time.
nodes.languageNotes: The empty `<script id="alc-payload" type="application/json">` is a Vite-friendly server-injection slot — the placeholder JSON is rewritten by the Python dashboard server before serving the bundled HTML.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Frontend manifest for the `alc-dashboard` React SPA: declares Vite/TypeScript build scripts plus React 18, Radix UI primitives, Recharts, Tailwind utilities, and `vite-plugin-singlefile` for emitting a single self-contained HTML bundle.
nodes.languageNotes: `vite-plugin-singlefile` is the key signal that the dashboard ships as one inlined HTML file consumed by the Python server — no separate JS/CSS asset routes.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Solution-style root TypeScript config that owns no files itself and only project-references `tsconfig.app.json` (SPA sources) and `tsconfig.node.json` (Vite build tooling).

nodes.name: tsconfig.app.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler config for the dashboard `src/` SPA sources: strict ES2022 + DOM with `react-jsx`, bundler module resolution, `@/*` path alias, and `noEmit` (Vite handles emit).
nodes.languageNotes: `allowImportingTsExtensions` plus `noEmit` is the modern Vite + TS pattern — type-check only, let the bundler do the work.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler config scoped to the Node-side build tooling — currently just `vite.config.ts` — with ES2023 lib, `node` types, and `noEmit`.

nodes.name: u11-apply.json
nodes.summary: Data contract for the U11 apply stage declaring two append-only SQLite events (patch_applied, patch_reverted) produced by alc_apply with 180-day retention and dashboard surfacing.
nodes.languageNotes: Manifest entries follow the producer/consumers/lifecycle convention shared across the U-stage contracts; events.sqlite is the canonical sink.

nodes.name: u12-invoke.json
nodes.summary: Data contract for the U12 invoke stage declaring the dev-agent markdown archive (repos/*/alc-agents/dev/*.md) with a 30-day retention and 512-entry cap.

nodes.name: u13-eval.json
nodes.summary: Data contract for the U13 eval stage defining the eval_verdict event-v4 JSONL payload (approve/reject/modify) and the seeded rec-quality-judge agent markdown.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds an inline JSON Schema for the eval_verdict payload (required verdict, judge_reason ≤200 chars, recommendation_kind) — the only manifest in this batch carrying an inline payload schema.

nodes.name: u8-analyst.json
nodes.summary: Data contract for the U8 analyst stage declaring four JSON artifacts — patterns, anomalies, correlations, and recommendations — each capped at 1 MiB and surfaced in the dashboard.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest manifest in the batch; each artifact has a distinct producer function (analyst_patterns/anomalies/correlations/score) flowing into the recommender_render consumer.

nodes.name: u9-recommender.json
nodes.summary: Data contract for the U9 recommender stage declaring patch-bundle JSON files (patches/*.json, 30-day retention) and a transient suggestions.json with a 7-day window.

nodes.name: RESULTS.md
nodes.summary: Phase A spike results log capturing baseline test snapshot, validation gate outcomes (G0.5.1 premise, G0.5.2 cross-runtime, G0.5.3 schema discovery), and the resulting go/no-go decision for the ALC plugin rewrite Phase B.
nodes.languageNotes: Spike result document organized by validation gate (G0.5.1/2/3) with operator-graded PASS/FAIL verdicts.

nodes.name: spike_validate_premise.sh
nodes.summary: G0.5.1 premise-validation driver that runs the specialist analyst against the operator's real ~/.claude/projects corpus and prints the top-10 recommendations in a grading-friendly format to decide whether the rewrite ships the full analyst or collapses to synthesizer-plus-nudges.
nodes.languageNotes: Bash with strict mode (set -euo pipefail); uses preflight existence checks, mktemp scratch dir, and inline heredoc Python for the anomaly probe.

nodes.name: spike_validate_runtime.sh
nodes.summary: G0.5.2 cross-runtime assumption checker that probes CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT availability, Codex .codex-plugin/ discovery, and Codex AGENTS.md auto-load behaviour to drive the conditional Codex scope decision in unit U3.
nodes.languageNotes: Three sequential probe checks emitting GREEN / INDIRECT / RED / UNKNOWN verdicts for operator grading.

nodes.name: spike_validate_schema.sh
nodes.summary: G0.5.3 schema-discovery script that locates existing hook-events.jsonl telemetry streams and verifies the {id, cost, tokens, duration_s} contract is recoverable, confirming whether the U5.5 v3→v4 migration has a real corpus to target.
nodes.languageNotes: Bash discovery script combining find over $HOME with embedded python3 for JSONL field sampling.

nodes.summary: Claude Code plugin descriptor for agent-learning-compounder declaring name, version, description, author, homepage, repository, and keywords.

nodes.summary: Empty package marker that makes alc_mcp/tests importable as a Python package for unittest discovery.

nodes.name: report-template
nodes.summary: Canonical agent-learning report markdown template with sections for confirmed_current, memory_derived, needs_verification, agent/skill compensation, self-healing loop, skill health, proposed evergreen diffs, and report metadata.
nodes.languageNotes: Extensionless canonical file paired with the report-template.md symlink — edits go here, not the .md alias.

nodes.name: report-template.md
nodes.summary: Markdown-suffixed symlink to assets/report-template — the presentation alias that downstream tools open when they expect a .md extension.

nodes.name: alc_apply
nodes.summary: Canonical executable CLI that applies a queued patch by id — validates the patch id, loads the operation from disk, and delegates to the dispatch executor under an apply lock.
nodes.languageNotes: Extensionless canonical script; alc_apply.py is a symlink to this file so Python's import machinery can resolve it as a module.

nodes.name: alc_apply.py
nodes.summary: Python-import-friendly symlink to bin/alc_apply exposing the same CLI as a module for cross-script imports.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry that parses args, validates the patch id, loads the queued op, and executes apply via HermesExecutor inside an apply lock, printing the resulting hashes as JSON.

nodes.name: _load_op
nodes.summary: Reads a queued patch op JSON by id from the repo's improvement queue staging directory, validating shape before returning.

nodes.name: alc_apply_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Core apply/revert executor — symlink-aware target resolution, sha256 preflight checks, atomic write with fsync+os.replace, flock-guarded apply lock, and JSONL event emission for both apply and revert.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements durable-write safety: rejects symlink targets, validates SHA256 preflight, scrubs secrets, and writes events through deterministic-id JSONL append.

nodes.name: HermesExecutor
nodes.summary: Executor that resolves a patch op to a writable target, applies the byte transform under flock, scrubs and base64-stores the original bytes for revert, and emits canonical apply/revert events to events.jsonl.

nodes.name: _apply_op_to_bytes
nodes.summary: Pure byte-level transform — supports replace (text-encoded), write (raw bytes from b64 or content), and single-anchor swap operations, raising ApplyError on ambiguity.

nodes.name: _apply_lock
nodes.summary: Context manager that acquires an exclusive fcntl flock with a bounded busy-wait so concurrent apply calls serialize cleanly per repo state directory.

nodes.name: _atomic_write
nodes.summary: Crash-safe write helper that stages bytes through a mkstemp file, fsyncs, and os.replaces onto the target path.

nodes.name: _check_no_symlink
nodes.summary: Refuses to apply through any symlink ancestor on the target path, preserving the installer trust boundary.

nodes.name: _load_event_payload
nodes.summary: Scans the repo events.jsonl to recover the payload of a previously emitted apply event, used by revert to restore original bytes.

nodes.name: alc_eval
nodes.summary: CLI that runs the ALC evaluation suite against fixture corpora and produces a scored report on recommender/analyst output quality.

nodes.name: alc_init
nodes.summary: Per-repo bootstrap CLI that writes .agent-learning.json, scaffolds repo state directories, and runs the self-test that validates the durable-write health contract.

nodes.name: alc_invoke
nodes.summary: Canonical dispatch CLI that invokes a Claude/Codex subagent by ref against a task prompt, emitting start/end events with prompt hashes and bounded telemetry while routing through a sandbox or mock backend.
nodes.languageNotes: Extensionless canonical executable paired with alc_invoke.py symlink for Python import resolution.

nodes.name: alc_invoke.py
nodes.summary: Python-importable symlink to bin/alc_invoke — the same dispatch CLI exposed under a .py extension so other scripts can import its functions.

nodes.name: DispatchResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding the result of a subagent dispatch — backend identity, model, outcome, telemetry summary, and any captured output path.

nodes.name: invoke
nodes.summary: Main library entry — resolves the agent ref to a concrete agent file, emits a hashed start event, dispatches via sandbox/mock backend with optional depth, then emits an end event with bounded telemetry.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI wrapper that parses args, resolves the state handle, and calls invoke() — printing the DispatchResult as JSON for downstream consumers.

nodes.name: _exec_sandbox_dispatch
nodes.summary: Dispatches the task into a real exec_sandbox backend, capturing token estimates, output path, and outcome metadata before returning a DispatchResult.

nodes.name: _dispatch
nodes.summary: Backend selector that routes to either the mock dispatcher or the exec_sandbox path based on environment and explicit sandbox-depth signal.

nodes.name: _resolve_agent_path
nodes.summary: Resolves an agent ref (name or path) to an absolute on-disk agent file by searching personal archive roots and the repo, with descriptive errors when unresolved.

nodes.name: _emit_start
nodes.summary: Writes a v4 dispatch-start event with the agent path, hashed task prompt, and actor — returning the event id for the matching _emit_end correlation.

nodes.name: _emit_end
nodes.summary: Writes a v4 dispatch-end event correlated to a parent start id, carrying bounded telemetry (token estimate, outcome status).

nodes.name: _read_frontmatter
nodes.summary: Parses leading YAML-style frontmatter from an agent markdown file, returning a dict of declared metadata used for dispatch decisions.

nodes.name: _cleanup_stale_dev_agents
nodes.summary: Prunes dev-archive agent files older than the configured retention window so the workspace stays bounded.

nodes.name: analyst_anomalies
nodes.summary: Analyst-pipeline stage that scans repo telemetry for anomalous patterns (spikes in correction rates, unusual gate failures) and writes findings into the analyst output.

nodes.name: analyst_correlations
nodes.summary: Analyst-pipeline stage that mines correlations between gate triggers and downstream correction events to support gate effectiveness scoring.

nodes.name: analyst_patterns
nodes.summary: Analyst-pipeline stage that mines recurring patterns across sessions (e.g. repeated correction n-grams) to feed domain-rule and gate proposals.

nodes.name: analyst_score
nodes.summary: Scoring stage of the analyst pipeline that ranks candidate gates/rules/recommendations and emits the final scored output consumed by the recommender.

nodes.name: auto_distill_session
nodes.summary: Automation entry that triggers a non-interactive distill_learning pass on the latest session transcripts and refreshes export surfaces.

nodes.name: backfill_transcripts
nodes.summary: Utility that backfills older session transcripts into the ingestion corpus so historical sessions contribute to baseline and learning state.

nodes.name: build_repo_baseline
nodes.summary: Ingestion command that scans the target repo and writes a structured baseline of repo facts (file inventory, key configs, language mix) for downstream analyst and distill stages.

nodes.name: causal_probe
nodes.summary: Runs deterministic A/B skip cohorts per gate to measure causal effect on correction rates — produces causal-effectiveness signals consumed by gate scoring.

nodes.name: ce_playbook
nodes.summary: Renders the Compound Engineering playbook view — surfaces gate inventory, recommendation backlog, and the operator's next-step playbook from current ALC state.

nodes.name: collect_hook_event
nodes.summary: CLI that appends a bounded, scrubbed hook event to the agent-learning JSONL telemetry log, rejecting symlinks and stripping secret markers before persisting.
nodes.languageNotes: Canonical shebanged Python executable (no .py extension); enforces the trust boundary against raw transcript persistence.

nodes.name: correlate_events
nodes.summary: Derives DAG edges from persisted hook events, building causal relationships between session steps for downstream analysis.

nodes.name: distill_learning
nodes.summary: Core ingestion step that compiles session evidence and repo baseline into a procedural-memory report; only mutates durable memory with explicit --write and --personal flags.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest script in the bin layer (974 lines); enforces the read-only default trust boundary.

nodes.name: evaluate_classifier
nodes.summary: Evaluates the domain-classifier's precision and recall against fixed fixture cases for regression detection.

nodes.name: evaluate_gate_effectiveness
nodes.summary: Computes correlation-only effectiveness signals per stable 12-char gate_id; intentionally avoids hard causal claims.

nodes.name: evaluate_skill_impact
nodes.summary: Quantifies skill-usage impact via correlation analysis rather than asserting causality.

nodes.name: evaluate_skill_routing
nodes.summary: Evaluates expected skill routing from scope text against the active skill map for routing-accuracy regression checks.

nodes.name: event_emit
nodes.summary: Thin CLI plus API wrapper for fire-and-forget background event emission used by other bin scripts.

nodes.name: exec_sandbox
nodes.summary: Tiered sandbox execution primitive that bounds untrusted code execution with configurable resource limits and profile-based policies.
nodes.languageNotes: Pairs with exec_sandbox_profiles.py for declarative profile definitions.

nodes.name: exec_sandbox_profiles.py
nodes.summary: Declarative profile definitions consumed by exec_sandbox to configure resource limits and policy tiers for bounded code execution.

nodes.name: export_gates
nodes.summary: Produces the durable compact latest-approved-gates.md surface from a validated agent-learning report; one of the two surfaces future agents load.

nodes.name: export_skill_context
nodes.summary: Produces the latest-skill-context.md compact surface listing active skills and health signals for future session-start loading.

nodes.name: extract_sessions
nodes.summary: Extracts text-bearing messages from agent JSONL transcripts, normalizing into session-scoped chunks for distillation.

nodes.name: extract_skill_usage
nodes.summary: Pulls expected, loaded, and applied skill-usage signals out of safe hook events for downstream skill-routing evaluation.

nodes.name: gates_inherit
nodes.summary: Inherits a gate from the shared cross-repo registry into a target repo's approved-gates set, recording derived_from provenance.

nodes.name: gates_promote
nodes.summary: Promotes a gate from a repo's approved-gates.md into the shared cross-repo registry to enable federation across projects.

nodes.name: index_events
nodes.summary: Incremental indexer that materializes events.jsonl into a queryable events.sqlite database using a checkpoint cursor.
nodes.languageNotes: Canonical executable; the .py sibling is a symlink to this file.

nodes.name: index_events.py
nodes.summary: Python-import-friendly symlink to bin/index_events, exposing indexer helpers like run() and parse_args() for cross-script import.
nodes.languageNotes: Per CLAUDE.md, this is a symlink to bin/index_events; the dual-name layout exists so Python's import machinery can resolve cross-script imports.

nodes.name: _resolve_state_path
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective state directory from explicit args, env, or repo defaults using the documented precedence order.

nodes.name: _extract_session_id
nodes.summary: Extracts a stable session id from a raw event payload, tolerating absent or malformed fields.

nodes.name: _event_to_row
nodes.summary: Converts a parsed event dict plus session id into the column tuple inserted into events.sqlite.

nodes.name: _ensure_schema
nodes.summary: Creates or migrates the SQLite schema and indexes if missing, making the indexer idempotent across restarts.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main indexing loop that streams events.jsonl from the cursor, parses rows, inserts into SQLite, and advances the checkpoint.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses argv, invokes run(), and reports the indexed-row count.

nodes.name: ingest_new_transcripts
nodes.summary: Live transcript ingest entry point that advances a cursor checkpoint to avoid reprocessing already-seen sessions.

nodes.name: init_learning_system
nodes.summary: Per-target-repo bootstrap that writes .agent-learning.json plus the state directory layout, and runs the layered health self-test.
nodes.languageNotes: Health contract anchor; new top-level state files must update run_self_test here.

nodes.name: install_runtime_hooks
nodes.summary: Two-phase runtime wiring step that installs Codex or Claude hook configs pointing at the shared learning collector; manifest-only without explicit --apply.
nodes.languageNotes: Adding a new runtime requires updating path handling, event mapping, command-integrity validation, and regression coverage here.

nodes.name: map_active_skills
nodes.summary: Discovers active Codex and Claude skills for a repo and computes basic health signals consumed by export_skill_context.

nodes.name: propose_domain_rules
nodes.summary: Mines corpus chunks for n-gram terms that co-occur with correction events, proposing candidate domain rules with statistical support.

nodes.name: queue_dedup
nodes.summary: Semantically collapses near-duplicate rows in improvement-queue.jsonl using stdlib trigrams or optional sentence-transformer embeddings.

nodes.name: recommender_generators.py
nodes.summary: Patch-bundle generators that turn validated recommendation records into agent-ready content for anomaly, routing, model-swap, agent-spawn, and workflow-chain kinds.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a kind-keyed dispatcher; each builder validates payload shape before emitting patch bundles.

nodes.name: ValidationError
nodes.summary: Sentinel exception raised by payload validators when a recommendation record fails schema checks.

nodes.name: _read_frontmatter
nodes.summary: Parses YAML-style frontmatter from a raw markdown blob, tolerating missing or malformed front-matter fences.

nodes.name: _validate_agent_content
nodes.summary: Validates an agent-content record against required structural rules, raising ValidationError on shape violations.

nodes.name: _validate_payload
nodes.summary: Dispatches payload validation by recommendation kind, enforcing per-kind required fields before generators run.

nodes.name: _build_anomaly_patch
nodes.summary: Builds the markdown patch heading and body for an anomaly-kind recommendation.

nodes.name: _build_routing_patch
nodes.summary: Builds the markdown patch for a skill-routing recommendation.

nodes.name: _build_model_swap_patch
nodes.summary: Builds a model-swap patch that updates the model line in agent frontmatter, preserving surrounding content.

nodes.name: alc-install.mjs
nodes.summary: npm/npx entry point that wraps install.sh, plus a workaround that materializes .py module aliases stripped by npm's symlink-dereferencing tarball packing.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Node's spawnSync to delegate to install.sh; idempotent file alias restoration runs before the shell handoff.

nodes.name: isPythonExecutable
nodes.summary: Detects whether a file is a shebanged, executable Python script that needs a .py alias materialized.

nodes.name: aliasLinkOrCopy
nodes.summary: Creates a symlink alias when supported, falling back to a file copy when the filesystem refuses symlinks (e.g. unpacked npm tarballs).

nodes.name: restoreSameDirAliases
nodes.summary: Restores bin/<name>.py aliases that npm strips during tarball packing, scoped to the same directory as the canonical executable.

nodes.name: restoreCrossDirAliases
nodes.summary: Restores scripts/<name>.py compatibility aliases that point to canonical bin/<name> executables after npm packing.

nodes.name: build_release.sh
nodes.summary: Reproducible release builder that stages a clean skill copy, sanitizes dev artifacts, and writes content-reproducible tar.gz and zip archives with fixed mtimes and sorted ordering.
nodes.languageNotes: Reproducibility achieved via sorted file ordering, fixed mtimes, numeric ownership, and a timestamp-free gzip header.

nodes.name: sanitize_skill_tree.sh
nodes.summary: Shared shell helper that strips dev artifacts (tests, caches, scratch files) from a staged copy of the skill tree before packaging.

nodes.name: sanitize_skill_tree
nodes.summary: Removes dev-only files from a target skill-tree path; sourced by build_release.sh.

nodes.name: repo_id
nodes.summary: Deterministic per-repo identifier: SHA-256 hex (first 12 chars) of the resolved absolute path, prefixed by the slugified directory name; used to scope state under <state-root>/repos/<repo-id>.

nodes.name: catalog.py
nodes.summary: Canonical MCP tool catalog declaring the 11 stdio tools (M1-M11) the ALC MCP server exposes — read/propose/observe/exec surfaces with parameter schemas, return schemas, and scope-aware (user/project/both) parameter wiring.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen dataclass MCPToolSpec; module-level _SCOPE_PARAM reused across read tools to enforce the user-vs-project scope contract.

nodes.name: MCPToolSpec
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass describing one MCP tool: id, kind, summary, backing implementation, JSON schemas for params/returns, examples, and version compatibility window.

nodes.name: alc_next_action.py
nodes.summary: Session-lifecycle synthesizer (M11 next_action) — collects bucketed signals via alc_query (pending patches, recommendations, recent applies, verdicts, cross-scope gate counts) and emits a structured recommendation per intent (start/next/end/recap/leftoff/auto) using a 5-rung priority ladder.
nodes.languageNotes: Library module — no CLI entry. The bin/state_handle fallback import block lets the module be invoked from outside the bin/ sys.path.

nodes.name: next_action
nodes.summary: Public entry point: normalises intent, collects signals, dispatches to the matching synthesis branch (start_next, end, recap, leftoff, auto), assembles the schema-conforming dict, and writes the latest-next-action.json cache.

nodes.name: _collect_signals
nodes.summary: Aggregates project-scope state (pending patches, recommendations, 7-day applies and verdicts) and user-scope cross-repo gate counts into the public signals dict — never returns raw rows.

nodes.name: _synthesise_start_next
nodes.summary: Forward-looking synthesis: walks the 5-rung priority ladder (pending patches → recent rejects → stale recs → recent commits → idle) and returns the first matching rung result.

nodes.name: _synthesise_end
nodes.summary: Session-close synthesis: chooses commit/PR, document-pending, or learn-from-quiet-session messaging based on recent applies and pending queue depth.

nodes.name: _synthesise_recap
nodes.summary: 7-day narrative synthesis summarising patch applies and verdict tallies with last-activity date and a pointer to the full learning report.

nodes.name: _synthesise_leftoff
nodes.summary: Resume-from-last-session synthesis: surfaces the first pending patch id and most recent activity to give the operator a clean re-entry point.

nodes.name: alc_query.py
nodes.summary: Canonical read-only query API (KTD-21) — the single seam through which MCP tools, dashboards, hooks, and CLI consume ALC state. Implements scope-aware (user/project/both) reads for gates, skill context, recommendations, pending patches, event DAGs, and skill-usage summaries.
nodes.languageNotes: All SQLite connections opened read-only via URI mode; the _with_conn contextmanager fixes a previously leaked-connection bug in the dashboard.

nodes.name: get_gates
nodes.summary: Scope-aware approved-gates reader (PR 2/3): merges project-scope and user-scope gate registers, dedupes by gate_id (project wins), tags each row with _source_scope, and optionally filters by domain.

nodes.name: get_skill_context
nodes.summary: Scope-aware skill-context markdown reader: returns project file, user file, or both concatenated under scope headers so consumers can distinguish cross-repo vs per-repo learning.

nodes.name: get_apply_log
nodes.summary: Patch-apply event reader from project-scope events.sqlite with optional since/kind filters; returns [] for user scope (gates are written there, not applied).

nodes.name: get_outcomes
nodes.summary: Eval-verdict event reader from project-scope events.sqlite with optional since filter.

nodes.name: get_recommendations
nodes.summary: Reads analyst recommendation rows from <reports>/recommendations.json (project-scope only).

nodes.name: get_pending_patches
nodes.summary: Returns pending patch bundles under <repo-state>/patches sorted by ts, filtering out deferred and rejected statuses; project-scope only.

nodes.name: get_event_dag
nodes.summary: Recursive CTE that reconstructs the parent/child event DAG for a session_id, capped at depth 64; falls back to orphan roots when a session's root chain is broken.

nodes.name: get_actor_summary
nodes.summary: Per-actor-kind event totals since a duration cutoff, grouped with unique-actor counts.

nodes.name: get_skill_usage_summary
nodes.summary: Aggregates events by actor_name with count, last-used timestamp, optional prefix filter — the closest skill-usage proxy in the events schema.

nodes.name: get_skill_invocation_history
nodes.summary: Returns per-actor invocation history from events.sqlite with a JSONL fallback for legacy events stores that persisted a skill field.

nodes.name: _parse_since
nodes.summary: Parses since= filter values across multiple types: None, ISO strings, epoch numbers, datetime, or short duration strings (e.g. 7d, 24h, 30m) into a UTC ISO cutoff string.

nodes.name: state_handle.py
nodes.summary: Canonical state-root resolver: maps repo paths to all ALC on-disk locations using the documented 7-tier precedence chain, with for_user() and for_project() constructors disambiguating cross-repo vs per-repo scope (PR 1 of the scope-rename rollout).
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen dataclass for StateHandle; sha256-prefixed repo-id slug keeps state dirs collision-free across renamed repos.

nodes.name: StateHandle
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding the resolved project-scope paths for a repo (state_root, repo_state_dir, reports_dir, dashboard_dir, alc_agents_dirs, apply log, outcomes, events). Class methods for_repo/for_project/for_user are the public constructors; for_repo additionally honours .agent-learning.json's state_dir override.

nodes.name: dashboard_url
nodes.summary: Returns the dashboard URL for a repo — prefers the localhost URL recorded in dashboard/server.json, otherwise falls back to the local index.html file URI.

nodes.name: _resolve_state_root
nodes.summary: Implements the documented 7-tier resolver precedence: explicit state_dir → AGENT_LEARNING_STATE_DIR → user/personal arg → AGENT_LEARNING_USER env → repo/.agent-learning → XDG_STATE_HOME → ~/.local/state.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI dashboard app builder that serves the pre-built React/Vite bundle with live data injection plus a JSON action API (/api/data, /api/health, /api/actions/distill, /api/actions/promote, /api/reports/latest). PR 3 added scope-aware gate surfacing under /api/data.scoped_gates.
nodes.languageNotes: Optional fastapi import with _FASTAPI_AVAILABLE guard; Pydantic request models defined only inside the try block. Job registry uses thread-safe locking for the backgrounded auto_distill_session subprocess.

nodes.name: JobRegistry
nodes.summary: Thread-safe in-memory registry of background dashboard jobs (e.g. distill runs) with bounded 200-line log tail and 20-job listing window.

nodes.name: build_app
nodes.summary: Constructs and returns the FastAPI app, wires all read/write endpoints, and inlines _build_scoped_gates() which delegates to alc_query for both project and user scope (PR 3) so the React SPA can render the dual-scope tabs.

nodes.name: server.py
nodes.summary: Read-only stdlib HTTP server for the Alpine dashboard skill — serves /, /data.json, and /static/<file>. All POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH return 405; writes stay on the CLI. PR 3 updated build_data_blob to call alc_query.get_gates(scope='both') and embed gates_rows + gates_summary for the single-list-with-badge UI.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses stdlib http.server + ThreadingMixIn rather than FastAPI so the skill stays dependency-free; sys.path bootstrap mirrors alc_init/alc_mcp.

nodes.name: _DashboardHandler

nodes.name: build_data_blob

nodes.name: create_server

nodes.name: _resolve_state

nodes.name: app.js
nodes.summary: Alpine dashboard client-side renderer — reads the inlined dashboard-data JSON, renders each tab panel (recommendations, patches, anomalies, gates, suggestions, etc.), and wires keyboard-accessible tab navigation. PR 3 added renderGatesList with scope badges so user vs project gates render in a single list with badge.

nodes.name: escapeHtml
nodes.summary: HTML-escapes a value against the HTML_ESCAPE_MAP — the security boundary for all dashboard innerHTML writes.

nodes.name: toRows

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level React component for the ALC dashboard; reads the injected payload, polls /api/data every 25s when online, and lays out KPI strip, trend/heat, gate stability, scoped gates, skill telemetry, and footer sections.

nodes.name: App

nodes.name: applyStoredTheme

nodes.name: trendSeries

nodes.name: AgentLearningCompounderTests
nodes.summary: TestCase aggregating ~38 end-to-end checks of the scripts/ CLI surface (extract, baseline, validate, distill, HTML report).

nodes.languageNotes: Cleanly skipTest()s when fastapi/jinja2 extras are not installed.

nodes.name: Dashboard
nodes.summary: TestCase that seeds a fixture repo with config/baseline/domain-rules/skill-map and reports, builds the FastAPI app via dashboard.build_app, and exercises HTTP routes — including the PR 3 scoped_gates contract.

nodes.name: test_alc_invoke.py
nodes.summary: Smoke + behavioral tests for the alc_invoke bin script: agent frontmatter validation, archive scoping (dev vs. test/eval), cleanup of older dev agents, structured invoke results, and forwarding of sandbox depth to exec_sandbox.

nodes.name: AlcInvokeTests
nodes.summary: TestCase covering alc_invoke's agent-frontmatter validation, archive layout, cleanup policy, and exec_sandbox forwarding.

nodes.name: _make_agent_text
nodes.summary: Test helper that produces a syntactically valid agent markdown body with frontmatter (name, description, color, model) and four block sections.

nodes.name: test_dashboard_readonly.py

nodes.languageNotes: Loads server.py and render_unified_report.py via SourceFileLoader to avoid placing them on sys.path; skips network tests on sandboxed loopback failures.

nodes.name: DashboardReadonlyTests
nodes.summary: TestCase that boots the alc-dashboard HTTP server against a tempdir StateHandle and asserts readonly contract + scope surfacing.

nodes.name: _start_server
nodes.summary: Test helper that boots SERVER.create_server in a daemon thread and skipTests when the loopback bind fails.

nodes.name: _http_get
nodes.summary: urllib helper that issues an HTTP request and converts loopback-blocked sandbox errors into unittest skips.

nodes.name: event_schema.py
nodes.summary: Defines the EventV4 dataclass plus ActorInfo, Telemetry, and ChainLink companions, with coercers, ISO-8601 validation, boundary enforcement, and JSON-Schema/SQLite-DDL generators that all hook telemetry rows must conform to.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python dataclasses with classmethods for parsing/coercion and reflects field types via typing.get_origin/get_args to emit JSON schemas at runtime.

nodes.name: ActorInfo
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing who emitted an event: actor kind (operator/agent/system), display name, optional model, and parent actor id for chained agent calls.

nodes.name: Telemetry
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding optional per-event timing/token/cost metrics (duration, tokens in/out, cache reads, creation tokens, cost USD, interrupted flag).

nodes.name: ChainLink
nodes.summary: Single entry in an event's correlation chain pairing a role (parent/child/sibling) with an event_id.

nodes.name: EventV4
nodes.summary: Canonical v4 hook-event record with deterministic id derivation, JSON-schema/SQLite-DDL emitters, lossless from_dict/to_dict round-tripping, and a v3->v4 upgrade_from migration path.

nodes.name: _enforce_boundary
nodes.summary: Walks all string fields in a payload and rejects values containing absolute paths, secret markers, transcript chunks, or oversized base64 blobs to enforce the telemetry trust boundary.

nodes.name: _json_type_for_type
nodes.summary: Reflects a Python type annotation (including Optional, Union, list, dict, dataclass) into a JSON-Schema fragment for runtime schema emission.

nodes.name: event_writer.py
nodes.summary: Durable write path for hook telemetry events: coerces rows through EventV4, scrubs secrets, enforces trust boundaries, takes a state-file flock, appends to events.jsonl with fsync, and rotates oversized logs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses fcntl.flock for cross-process state locking and rejects symlinked/non-regular event files to harden the durable-write path.

nodes.name: _acquire_state_lock
nodes.summary: Context manager that opens the per-state lock file and holds an fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX) for the duration of an event-write transaction.

nodes.name: _scrub_strings
nodes.summary: Recursively walks a value and applies the secret-scrubber to every string leaf, raising on patterns the scrubber rejects.

nodes.name: _boundary_checks
nodes.summary: Post-scrub second-pass rejecting events whose string fields still contain absolute paths, secret markers, transcript chunks, base64 blobs, or blob-key field names.

nodes.name: _coerce_row
nodes.summary: Normalizes a raw dict or EventV4 dataclass into a write-ready row: assigns event_id/ts defaults, routes through EventV4 validation when applicable, runs boundary checks, scrubs strings, and bounds text length.

nodes.name: write_event
nodes.summary: Public single-event entrypoint that wraps write_events_batch for one row, returning the resolved event path.

nodes.name: write_events_batch
nodes.summary: Coerces every row, takes the state lock, ensures the events.jsonl path is a regular file, appends one JSON line per event with fsync, and triggers post-write rotation.

nodes.name: _rotate_if_needed
nodes.summary: Rotates the events log to a timestamped sibling when it exceeds the configured byte threshold and re-chmods the new file to 0600.

nodes.name: sandbox_run_state.py
nodes.summary: SQLite-backed tracker for sandbox exec runs: records per-run status, owns the worktree directory lifecycle, and recovers stale rows whose owning PID is gone by cleaning the worktree and emitting a recovery event.
nodes.languageNotes: Stores run rows in SQLite with WAL journaling and uses os.kill(pid, 0) liveness probes to drive stale-run recovery.

nodes.name: RunStateTracker
nodes.summary: Encapsulates the run-state SQLite database: opens/closes the connection, sets/reads/deletes per-exec-id status rows, allocates worktree directories, and runs the stale-recovery sweep.

nodes.name: _cleanup_worktree
nodes.summary: Safely removes a sandbox worktree directory, refusing paths outside the repo's worktrees root to prevent accidental rm -rf of unrelated trees.

nodes.name: _emit_recovered_event
nodes.summary: Emits a sandbox.run.recovered event via the event_writer when stale-recovery cleans an abandoned exec, including the exec_id, worktree, and acting actor.

nodes.name: test_exec_sandbox_eval.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for exec-sandbox eval scope: recursion-depth guards, mocked alc invocation, and parent-event chain propagation.

nodes.name: ExecSandboxEvalTests
nodes.summary: unittest.TestCase covering recursion guard, mocked alc invoke, and parent-event chain handling for the exec_sandbox eval entrypoint.

nodes.name: test_exec_sandbox_read.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for exec-sandbox read scope: allowlisted command execution, forbidden-command blocking (exit 3), timeout exit 124, and no-network flag propagation.

nodes.name: ExecSandboxReadTests
nodes.summary: unittest.TestCase covering allowlist, forbidden-block, timeout, and no-network behaviors of the exec_sandbox read scope.

nodes.name: test_exec_sandbox_security.py
nodes.summary: Security tests for the exec sandbox: write-outside-repo blocking, path-traversal rejection, shell-metacharacter bypass blocking, network env sanitization, secret scrubbing in event payloads, and stdout truncation to 100KB.

nodes.name: ExecSandboxSecurityTests
nodes.summary: unittest.TestCase asserting the exec sandbox's defense-in-depth controls: traversal, metachar, network, secret, and payload-size boundaries.

nodes.name: test_exec_sandbox_worktree.py
nodes.summary: Tests the exec-sandbox worktree lifecycle: per-run write/clean, timeout-still-cleans, concurrent-run isolation, and stale-recovery worktree cleanup.

nodes.name: ExecSandboxWorktreeTests
nodes.summary: unittest.TestCase exercising worktree allocation, cleanup on timeout, concurrent isolation, and stale-run recovery against the RunStateTracker.

nodes.name: test_pipeline_b1_b3_b12.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end pipeline regression covering bugs B1/B3/B12: drives collect_hook_event -> event_writer -> index_events -> alc_query against a tmpdir StateHandle to catch schema-bump drift between collector and indexer.

layers.name: Install & Distribution
layers.description: Install orchestration (install.sh, bootstrap.sh, alc-install.mjs), marketplace and plugin manifests, package metadata, optional-dependency declarations — everything that lets a fresh repo or runtime adopt the skill.

layers.name: Pipeline Core
layers.description: The canonical Python pipeline that distills repo facts, sessions, and telemetry into durable agent memory — bin/ canonical executables plus their scripts/ symlinks (analyst, recommender, gate exports, hook collection, refresh).

layers.name: MCP Server
layers.description: Stdio MCP server (alc_mcp/) exposing get_gates, report_outcome, propose_gate, and get_skill_context to consuming agents, with its catalog and README.

layers.name: Dashboard (FastAPI + React SPA)
layers.description: Operator dashboard: FastAPI/Jinja2/HTMX server (dashboard/), the React+Vite+Tailwind SPA shell under dashboard/web/, and the alc-dashboard skill package that wraps it for plugin consumption.

layers.name: Data Contracts
layers.description: JSON-schema manifests for U8–U13 pipeline stages (analyst, recommender, apply, invoke, eval) plus the shared base schema — the durable contract between pipeline stages and downstream consumers.

layers.name: Runtime Integration
layers.description: Surfaces that bind the skill into Codex/Claude runtimes: hook manifests, slash-command definitions, agent persona registrations, domain-rule presets, report-template assets, and the bundled alc-core / alc-next SKILL manifests.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Both test suites — fixtures/tests/ (the real unit+integration suite, ~166 tests) and tests/ (post-install smoke suite verifying installed layout), plus alc_mcp/tests/ for MCP-specific coverage.

layers.name: Documentation & References
layers.description: Project docs (README, ARCHITECTURE, STRATEGY, CONTEXT, CHANGES, CLAUDE.md), the docs/ tree of decision records and dev audits, and the canonical reference-lib/ markdown (mirrored into skills/alc-core/references/ via symlinks).

tour.title: What ALC Is
tour.description: Start with the README to internalise the project's single-sentence pitch: a compound-memory loop where sessions feed durable agent memory and future sessions read it back. The README maps out the three first-class install paths (npx, curl-pipe-sh, Claude marketplace), the auto-loaded compact-context files that an agent sees at session start, and the trust posture — no raw prompts, transcripts, or tool output ever land on disk. ARCHITECTURE.md is the deeper companion: it names the eight layers and the KTD seam abstractions (alc_query, alc_propose, alc_apply, exec_sandbox) you'll meet later in the tour.

tour.title: Three Install Paths, One Manifest
tour.description: ALC ships as a portable skill package, and the three install entry points all converge on the same payload. `package.json` exposes the `alc-install` npx shim, `marketplace.json` is the Claude Code marketplace descriptor, and `MANIFEST.json` is the authoritative declaration of version `2026.05.27+review7-plus2.2`, install roots, the 14 script entry points, and which optional extras (MCP server, dashboard, embedding dedup) are available. Treat MANIFEST.json as the contract every install path must satisfy.

tour.title: The Install Layer in Code
tour.description: Three thin programs implement the three install paths and they all delegate to one place. `scripts/alc-install.mjs` is the npm/npx entry that also works around npm's tarball symlink-dereferencing by re-materialising the dual-name `.py` aliases. `bootstrap.sh` is the curl-pipe-sh script that fetches the tarball and execs the real installer. `install.sh` is that real installer — it resolves the target runtime (Codex, Claude, or both), refuses to write through symlinks, backs existing installs into timestamped `.bak-<ts>` directories, and is the only thing that ever touches the install root.
tour.languageLesson: POSIX shell installers prefer `set -eu` and explicit `command -v` probes over assumed-present tooling so they degrade predictably on stripped CI containers and old macOS shells.

tour.title: Per-Repo Bootstrap and First-Run Profiling
tour.description: Once the skill is installed, each consuming repo needs its own state and self-test. `bin/init_learning_system` writes `<repo>/.agent-learning.json`, scaffolds the state directory tree, and runs the durable-write health contract that future stages depend on. `bin/alc_init` is the agent-facing first-run profiler — it brings the MCP surface to green and is what an agent invokes the very first time it lands in a fresh repo. Both are deliberately declarative: they never register schedulers or touch tracked files.

tour.title: Wiring Runtime Hooks
tour.description: Hook telemetry is what closes the compound loop — but installing it touches the user's runtime config, so it's deliberately two-phase. `install_runtime_hooks.py` is a `--dry-run` by default; you must pass `--apply` to actually write `.codex/hooks.json` or `.claude/settings.local.json`. It validates command integrity, confirms the manifest path is gitignored, and refuses to overwrite if the file is already tracked. This is the seam where the installer hands telemetry off to the runtime.

tour.title: Telemetry Capture — From Hook Payload to JSONL
tour.description: When a hook fires, three modules collaborate to write a single trustworthy event. `collect_hook_event.py` is the runtime-agnostic adapter: it normalises raw Codex/Claude payloads into the bounded allowlist schema. `event_schema.py` is the canonical event-v4 contract (ActorInfo, Telemetry, ChainLink, deterministic IDs, JSON-schema + SQLite-DDL emission). `event_writer.py` is the chokepoint — it enforces the trust boundary: flock-based state lock, regular-file-only writes, secret scrubbing, length bounding, and atomic JSONL rotation. Any new caller writing telemetry must go through this trio.
tour.languageLesson: Python dataclasses with `__post_init__` deterministic ID computation give you both schema enforcement and stable cross-process equality without pulling in a runtime validator like Pydantic — useful when the writer must work in stripped-down install environments.

tour.title: Trust Seams — Scrubbing, Validation, State Paths
tour.description: Trust in ALC is enforced by three specific files that every writer and exporter routes through. `scrub_secrets.py` (the top fan-in module in the whole repo) is the single chokepoint that redacts tokens, keys, paths, and email addresses before anything lands on disk. `validate_outputs.py` rejects psychological/ability claims about the operator and verifies bucket-line shape before durable-memory writes — set `AGENT_LEARNING_SUBJECT_NAMES` to catch personal-name variants. `state_paths.py` resolves the documented state-root precedence and provides atomic write helpers that refuse to follow symlinks. Treat these three as the load-bearing trust seams of the whole project.

tour.title: Ingestion — Repo Facts and Session Transcripts
tour.description: With trustworthy event capture in place, the ingestion stage turns inputs into a corpus. `build_repo_baseline.py` walks the target repo and extracts purpose, stack, entry points, CI files, packaging commands, and instruction rules — the static side of the corpus. `extract_sessions.py` walks the runtime's project directories, samples middle messages from session transcripts, and filters by working-directory match — the dynamic side. Together they produce the input that the analyst and distillation pipeline consume next.

tour.title: The Analyst and Recommender Pipeline
tour.description: This is the brain. `analyst_queries.py` is the catalogue of the 10 canonical Q1-Q10 SQL queries that run read-only against the v4 events DB with a schema-version guard — it produces the patterns, anomalies, correlations, and recommendations artifacts. `recommender_generators.py` then turns validated recommendation records into agent-ready patch bundles for the anomaly, routing, model-swap, agent-spawn, and workflow-chain kinds. The data contracts in `u8-analyst.json` and `u9-recommender.json` are the durable JSON-schema manifests pinning each artifact's shape, retention, and surfacing rules — change those manifests and every consumer downstream must follow.

tour.title: Distillation and Refresh — Closing the Loop
tour.description: `distill_learning.py` is the core distillation pipeline that turns the corpus into a durable learning report with gates, skill sections, and evergreen proposals. `refresh_learning_state.py` is the top-level orchestrator that chains baseline, skill-usage, gate-effectiveness, domain-rule mining, and queue-dedup steps under a single state lock — re-derive everything, declaratively, never registering a scheduler. `auto_distill_session` is the non-interactive automation entry that runs after a session ends, refreshing the export surfaces an agent will load on next start. This is the loop the README's diagram closes.

tour.title: Compact-Context Exports — What an Agent Sees on Start
tour.description: An agent never reads the raw corpus; it reads two synthesised markdown files plus a session digest. `export_gates.py` renders `latest-approved-gates.md` from the gate registry — it parses gate blocks, applies probes, and preserves inherited blocks from the shared cross-repo registry (note the `derived_from:` provenance). `export_skill_context.py` renders `latest-skill-context.md`, the compact skill-routing surface that replaces the raw skill map. These two markdown files are what auto-loads at session start; the README calls them out by name for a reason.

tour.title: The MCP Surface — 12 Tools, One Catalog
tour.description: Live querying of the durable memory happens via the stdio MCP server. `alc_mcp/catalog.py` is the canonical `MCP_TOOLS` catalog defining the M1-M11 capability specs split across read (`get_gates`, `get_recommendations`, `get_skill_context`), propose (`propose_gate`, `report_outcome`), sandbox (`exec_sandbox`), and the newest synthesiser `next_action` (M11). `server.py` is the JSON-RPC stdio server — it auto-registers every catalog entry, wiring each `MCPToolSpec.backing` into a thin async handler over alc_query/alc_propose. `.mcp.json` is the manifest that Claude Code reads to discover all of this. Add a tool by appending to the catalog; the server picks it up automatically.
tour.languageLesson: Catalog-driven RPC servers separate the contract (the data-defined spec) from the dispatcher (the auto-wired handler). Adding a tool becomes one data entry rather than three coordinated edits — a pattern worth copying whenever you have N similar endpoints.

tour.title: Next-Action Synthesis — One Source of Truth
tour.description: `alc_next_action.py` is the M11 capability the README highlights as 'the single best move from current state.' It walks signal rungs — pending patches, recent rejects, stale recommendations, idle — and produces intent-specific suggestions for session-start, session-end recap, and the standalone 'what's next?' query. The README's claim that one synthesiser handles all three intents lives here; if you ever feel tempted to write a separate end-of-session summariser, look here first.

tour.title: The Dashboard — FastAPI Shell, React SPA
tour.description: The operator-facing surface is two complementary layers. `skills/alc-dashboard/server.py` is the localhost FastAPI/Jinja2/HTMX server that reads suggestions off the state surface and buckets recommendations for display — the server-rendered baseline that works without a build step. The React SPA at `dashboard/web/src/main.tsx` and `App.tsx` is the richer surface: a Vite + Tailwind app that applies a stored theme, polls the backend, and composes the action bar plus domain-heat panels. Both consume the same backend data; you choose the surface by which entry point you run.

tour.title: Where to Extend — Catalogs, Contracts, Plugin Surfaces
tour.description: Three artifacts are the canonical extension points. The MCP catalog (Step 12) is where you add tools. The data-contract manifests under `data-contracts/manifests/` — the U11 apply contract is a good representative — are where you declare new pipeline-stage artifacts with retention, caps, and dashboard surfacing. `plugin.json` and the runtime entrypoints `AGENTS.md` (Codex) and `CLAUDE.md` (Claude) are where you register slash commands, persona agents, and implicit-invocation policy. Touch these three surfaces together and the change ripples cleanly through install, MCP, and the dashboard.
